data.title: Paysafe Shares Surge Over 16% on Takeover Buzz--Is a Major Fintech Deal on the Horizon?
data.description:
Paysafe is back in the spotlight, but not for the reasons investors might have hoped. The digital payments firm, which went public via a SPAC merger with Bill Foley's blank-check company, is reportedly exploring a sale after attracting takeover interest. Backed by private equity giants Blackstone and CVC Capital Partners, Paysafe is working with advisers to evaluate its options, including offloading non-core assets before any potential deal. The stock surged over 16% at 11.43am today, but let's not forgetthe company's market cap has cratered from its $9 billion SPAC valuation in 2020, to only $1.4 billion of market capitalization today.

The fintech space has been brutal, and Paysafe is a prime example of how things can unravel fast. Despite offering payment processing, digital wallets, and prepaid card services, the company has struggled to regain investor confidence. Now, with M&A heating up in the sector, a sale could be the best shot at salvaging value. That said, nothing is guaranteedBlackstone and CVC have kept quiet, and Paysafe itself hasn't commented yet.

For investors, this is a textbook case of what happens when SPAC hype meets reality. Paysafe's next move will be a crucial signal for the broader fintech sector, especially for other struggling players looking for a way out. If a deal materializes, it could reinforce the appetite for consolidation in the sector. If not, Paysafe will need to focus on stabilizing its business and charting a path forward. Either way, the company's trajectory underscores the volatility of SPAC-driven valuations and the challenges that come with sustaining long-term growth in the digital payments space.

data.title: Small Businesses Report Hiring Difficulties Persist in January, NFIB Says
data.description:
Small businesses reported that the level of worker shortages held steady in January, with a seasonally adjusted 35% of respondents in a National Federation of Independent Business survey released Thursday noting trouble hiring the workers that they needed, the same as in December.

NFIB said that openings were highest in the transportation, construction, and manufacturing sectors, and the lowest in the agriculture and finance sectors.

The percentage of business owners reporting labor quality as their top business concern fell to 18% from 19% in the previous month. Labor costs were listed as single most important concern by 9% of respondents, down from 11% in December.

About 52% of owners said they were trying to hire in the month, down from 55% in December. Around 90% of the respondents who were hiring or trying to hire reported finding few or no qualified applicants for the position they were trying to fill.

"Small business owners are certainly feeling hopeful about the direction of the economy," said Bill Dunkelberg, NFIB's chief economist. "However, employment remains a top concern as Main Street owners continue to face challenges in finding qualified employees to fill their open positions."

A seasonally adjusted net 33% of owners said they needed to raise compensation to hire workers in January, up from a nearly four-year low 29% in December. A net 20% of owners said they plan to raise compensation in the next three months, down from 24% in December.

The US Bureau of Labor Statistics' monthly employment report for January is scheduled to be released at 8:30 am ET Friday.

data.title: Immuneering Price Target Maintained With a $12.00/Share by Needham
Edison Says Its Equipment May Have Been Involved in SoCal Fires — Update
data.description:
By Dean Seal

Edison International's electric utility in Southern California said there may be a link between its equipment and two of the wildfires that raged in the area last month.

Southern California Edison on Thursday submitted two letters to the California Public Utilities Commission with updates on its analysis of the Eaton and Hurst wildfires, saying it believes its equipment may be associated with the start of the Hurst fire.

Information and data, including videos from external parties, also suggest the Edison unit's equipment was involved in the Eaton fire, though the utility hasn't found any typical indications, such as broken conductors, that would support such a link, the company said.

"While we do not yet know what caused the Eaton wildfire, SCE is exploring every possibility in its investigation, including the possibility that SCE's equipment was involved," Edison International Chief Executive Pedro Pizarro said.

Edison shares were down about 1% at $51.93 on Thursday. Shares have plunged 33% since the fires broke out on Jan. 7.

The Los Angeles Fire Department is still investigating both fires and suggested the link between the Edison unit's equipment and the Hurst fire, Edison said. The Sylmar-area blaze burned about 799 acres and was fully contained on Jan. 16.

The fire department's investigation into the Eaton fire is ongoing and is expected to take several months to complete, Edison said. The Eaton fire burned 14,021 acres and was fully contained about a week ago, having destroyed more than 6,000 homes and thousands of other structures.

The two blazes were among seven wildfires that devastated the L.A. area last month, killing more than two dozen people and wiping out thousands of homes and buildings. Analysts are forecasting up to $30 billion in insured losses from the fires, the largest total for any fire in the world in recent times.

Edison said in mid-January that its Southern California utility had filed two electric-safety reports regarding the Hurst and Eaton fires. The first, filed Jan. 9, said the unit found no operational issues with its power lines in relation to the Eaton fire, though it had still received preservation notices from insurance companies' legal teams, which could suggest potential liability.

The second report, filed a day later, said a downed power line was found near the Hurst fire, though the report didn't confirm whether the damage had occurred before or after the blaze started.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: Continuing Claims Show Slackening Job Market — Market Talk
data.description: Weekly jobless claims data haven't shown a sharp deterioration, but there's been a "slow bleed" all the same, Neil Dutta of Renaissance Macro notes. There has been very little movement at all in the initial-claims data series over the past 12 months, but the continuing-claims series is up about 5% over the past year, reflecting increasing difficulty that laid-off workers are facing finding a new job. "I am inclined to believe that there is still upside risk to the unemployment rate," Dutta concludes, ahead of Friday's January jobs report. The WSJ consensus projects unemployment will hold steady at 4.1%. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Skyworks, Qualcomm, Lilly, Honeywell, Ford, Roblox, Peloton, Amazon, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stocks were mostly higher Thursday as investors looked past worries over tariffs and turned their attention to falling bond yields and corporate earnings.

These stocks were moving Thursday:

Qualcomm posted fiscal first-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.75 a share, beating Wall Street estimates of $2.46, as revenue jumped 17% to $11.67 billion. Revenue at the company's QCT semiconductor business rose 20% in the period to $10.1 billion. But intellectual property licensing revenue of $1.54 billion missed estimates of $1.56 billion. Qualcomm said it expects fiscal second-quarter revenue of $2.38 to $2.58 a share on revenue of $10.3 billion to $11.2 billion. Shares of the chip maker were falling 4.8%.

Chip designer Arm Holdings reported fiscal third-quarter adjusted earnings that beat analysts' estimates but its guidance for its fourth quarter matched forecasts, disappointing investors and sending the stock down 4.5%. For the fourth quarter, Arm said it expects adjusted earnings of 52 cents a share on sales of $1.23 billion at the midpoint of its guidance. Wall Street anticipates fourth-quarter earnings of 52 cents a share on sales of $1.22 billion.

Skyworks Solutions plunged 24% after the wireless chip maker said it expected its share of the business to supply Apple, its biggest customer, with radio frequency components would decline by 20% to 25%. The drop, Skyworks said in a conference call, would begin impacting revenue in its fiscal fourth quarter and throughout fiscal 2026. The company also named Philip Brace, who most recently served as CEO of 5G mobile company Inseego, as its next president and CEO.

Honeywell International announced Thursday a plan to break into three companies in an effort to unlock shareholder value. The industrial conglomerate also reported solid fourth-quarter earnings but issued profit and revenue guidance for 2025 that was below analysts' estimates. Shares fell 5.7%.

Ford Motor dropped 5.9% after the auto maker posted fourth-quarter operating profit of $2.1 billion on sales of $48.2 billion, better than analysts' estimates on both metrics, but its forecast for operating profit this year of between $7 billion and $8.5 billion missed expectations of about $9.3 billion. Ford also said it expects breakeven adjusted earnings before interest and taxes in the first quarter because of "lower wholesales and unfavorable mix," and expenses related to the launch of some major U.S. assembly plants.

Eli Lilly, the drugmaker, posted fourth-quarter earnings that beat Wall Street expectations. Adjusted earnings in the period were $5.32 a share, better than estimates of $5.01. The company said it sees 2025 adjusted earnings in the range of $22.50 to $24 a share. The midpoint of the guidance tops analysts' expectations of $22.76. The stock was up 4.3%.

Bristol Myers Squibb declined 1.3% after fourth-quarter earnings soundly beat Wall Street consensus but management's guidance for the 2025 was short of expectations.

Roblox sank 12% after the online-gaming company posted fourth-quarter bookings and average daily active users that rose from a year earlier but missed Wall Street forecasts.

Peloton Interactive jumped 15% after the maker of at-home fitness equipment reported better-than-expected fiscal second-quarter revenue and boosted fiscal-year guidance for adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization to a range of $300 million to $350 million.

Tapestry was up 11%. The owner of Coach, Kate Spade, and Stuart Weitzman reported fiscal second-quarter earnings that beat Wall Street estimates and raised its fiscal-year outlook.

MicroStrategy, the largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, fell 2.7%. The company, which announced Wednesday it now was doing business as Strategy, posted a fourth-quarter loss of $670.8 million, or $3.03 a share. Revenue fell 3% to $120.7 million and missed analysts' estimates of $122.4 million. The company also said it saw its largest-ever increase in quarterly Bitcoin holdings in the period, "culminating in the acquisition of 218,887 bitcoins acquired for $20.5 billion" since the third quarter. The company also said it has so far bought $20 billion of Bitcoin under a three-year plan of buying $42 billion in the cryptocurrency.

Molina Healthcare fell 8.5% after fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $5.05 a share missed analysts' expectations of $5.88 and the healthcare-services provider said it expected 2025 adjusted earnings of at least $24.50 a share, below analysts' estimates of $25.82.

FormFactor, the provider of test and measurement technologies, was falling 12% after issuing a weak first-quarter forecast. The company said it expects adjusted earnings of 15 cents to 23 cents a share on revenue of $165 million to $175 million. Analysts had been expecting adjusted earnings of 33 cents a share on revenue of $192.9 million.

Meta Platforms was up for a 14th-straight session. The stock set its longest winning streak on record Wednesday, rising for 13 consecutive days. Shares of the parent of Facebook and Instagram also Wednesday set an intraday high of $718.14, and closed at a record high of $704.87. Meta was up 1.3% Thursday at $714.04.

Earnings reports are expected after the closing bell Thursday from Amazon.com, Fortinet, Cloudflare, and Take-Two Interactive Software.

Amazon.com was up 0.4%. Analysts expect the tech and online retailing giant to report fourth-quarter earnings of $1.49 a share on revenue of $187.3 billion. Wall Street will be watching Amazon's web services business closely after rival tech companies' cloud segments posted disappointing results. Amazon's report is scheduled for after the closing bell Thursday.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Gasoline margins rise, ARA stocks extend record
data.description:
Northwest European gasoline refining profit margins rose by $1.23 on Thursday to $11.62 a barrel, while gasoline inventories independently held in ARA extended a record high on slow exports.

  • A total of 3,000 metric tons of E5 gasoline traded, as TotalEnergies and Sahara sold barges to Mabanaft and Gunvor.

  • Another 4,000 tons of E10 gasoline traded as Glencore sold to Varo.

  • ARA gasoline stocks rose by 3.5% to extend a record-high at 1.69 million tons on slow exports, as traders have been benefiting from a contango structure to sell cargoes at higher prices in the future, Insights Global's Lars van Wageningen said.

  • ARA gasoline exports fell to their lowest in nearly five years in January at 419,000 barrels per day (bpd), down from 499,000 bpd in December, according to Kpler.

  • Meanwhile, EU and UK gasoline exports reached 895,000 bpd in January, Kpler data showed, down from 942,000 bpd in December.

data.title: Update: Huntington Ingalls Industries Shares Fall After Lower Q4 Results Miss Estimates
data.description:
(Updates with stock price movement in the headline and first paragraph.)

Huntington Ingalls Industries shares fell 17% in recent Thursday trading after the company reported lower Q4 earnings and revenue that were also below estimates.

The company reported Q4 earnings of $3.15 per diluted share, down from $6.90 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $3.38.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $3.0 billion, down from $3.18 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $3.05 billion.

The company said it expects mid- to long-term revenue to grow by over 4%.

data.title: LIQUIDITY SERVICES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Liquidity Services Inc., a leading provider of online marketplaces for surplus assets, has released its latest 10-Q report, showcasing significant growth in both financial and operational metrics. The company's robust performance is driven by increased revenues across various segments and improved operational efficiency.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $122.3 million, increased by 71.5% compared to the same period last year, driven by growth in purchase programs and expansion of service offerings.

Gross Profit: $50.2 million, reflecting a strong performance across segments, particularly in GovDeals and RSCG.

Income from Operations: $7.1 million, a significant increase of 330.3% from the previous year, indicating improved operational efficiency.

Net Income: $5.8 million, up 204.7% year-over-year, benefiting from higher revenues and controlled operating expenses.

Diluted EPS: $0.18, compared to $0.06 in the prior year, reflecting the company's enhanced profitability and effective cost management.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

  • GovDeals: Segment saw a 29.1% increase in total revenues, driven by an 11.4% increase in GMV due to higher personal property sales and an expanded seller base.
  • RSCG: Segment experienced a 100.5% increase in revenue, attributed to a 64.9% increase in GMV from expanded purchase programs.
  • CAG: Segment's revenue increased by 25.7%, with a 31.2% rise in GMV due to strong consignment sales in heavy equipment and energy categories.
  • Machinio: Revenue grew by 10.5% due to price increases and subscriber growth.

Geographical Performance

The percentage of revenues from transactions outside the United States decreased from 12.1% to 9.8% year-over-year, indicating a stronger performance in domestic markets compared to international ones.

Sales Units

The company completed 253,000 transactions in the three months ended December 31, 2024, compared to 239,000 in the same period in 2023, reflecting increased activity in its marketplaces.

Future Outlook

The company anticipates continued growth in purchase volumes, particularly in the RSCG segment, driven by general merchandise categories. It also expects temporary storage costs in the RSCG segment to normalize in the next quarter.

Macroeconomic Conditions

The company is monitoring the impact of inflation, heightened interest rates, and international geopolitical conflicts on its operations, although these factors have not materially affected its financial results to date.

Industry Trends

The company is benefiting from trends such as increased online and omni-channel retail, demand for sustainability solutions, and outsourcing of surplus disposition by corporations and government entities.

Operational Strategy

The company is focusing on expanding its service offerings and client base, particularly in the GovDeals segment through acquisitions like Sierra, and enhancing its marketplace platforms with new technologies.

SEC Filing: LIQUIDITY SERVICES INC [ LQDT ] - 10-Q - Feb. 06, 2025

data.title: Update: Philip Morris Shares Rise After Q4 Adjusted Earnings, Revenue Climb
data.description:
(Updates to add stock move in the headline and the first paragraph.)

Philip Morris International shares rose 10% in recent Thursday trading after the company reported higher Q4 adjusted earnings and revenue.

In the quarter ended Dec. 31, adjusted earnings rose to $1.55 per diluted share from $1.36 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $1.50.

Revenue climbed to $9.71 billion from $9.05 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $9.44 billion.

In Q1, the company expects adjusted diluted EPS of $1.58 to $1.63. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect $1.61.

The company expects 2025 adjusted EPS of $7.04 to $7.17. Analysts surveyed by FactSet forecast $6.99. The company projects net revenue growth of around 6% to 8% for the year. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $39.6 billion.

data.title: Uncle Sam’s debt offers flimsy refuge to investors
data.description:
Yields on 10-year US government bonds have dropped amid signs of slowing growth and President Donald Trump’s tariff talk. But the safe haven faces threats from $8 trln in unfinanced tax cuts, inflation, and Elon Musk’s meddling with payment systems. The calm is unlikely to last.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @Rubinations on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Yields on 10-year U.S. Treasury bonds declined to 4.42% on February 5 as investors weighed new growth concerns and the possibility of trade barriers with Canada and Mexico. The yield hit a recent high of 4.8% on January 13.

data.title: YC-backed &AI Raises $6.5 Million to Launch the First AI Agent for Patent Attorneys
data.description:
SAN FRANCISCO, Feb. 06, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — &AI, which provides the first AI agent for patent attorneys, announced today that it has raised a $6.5M seed round, led by First Round and supported by Y Combinator, SV Angel, BoxGroup and angels Kulveer Taggar, JJ Fliegelman and Rich Aberman. 

&AI provides a secure workspace engineered for patent attorneys, leveraging AI to accelerate end-to-end prosecution and litigation workflows. The &AI platform helps attorneys effectively manage patent portfolios by streamlining labor-intensive processes like prior art searches, claim chart drafting, and responding to office actions. Andy is an AI agent that takes this foundation further by collaborating with attorneys to utilize these tools, significantly improving efficiency and work quality.

The funding will allow &AI to enhance Andy's functionality, broaden integrations and grow its team of experts in AI and patent law. 

"Like many old-school industries, the legal space is ripe for AI disruption, and Caleb and the &AI team have built a very compelling offering that solves a big problem for law firms - accelerating patent due diligence," said Liz Wessel, Partner at First Round Capital. "&AI is already seeing great traction with customers, including Am Law 100 firms - I'm excited to be a part of the company's journey."

According to Caleb Harris, Co-founder and CEO, &AI has already had success selling into large law firms, with early customers using the platform to build thousands of claim charts for litigation, reporting an average time savings of 70-90 percent. Harris started the company with co-founder Herbert Turner - the two attended MIT together where they studied engineering. Harris later worked as a technical advisor to law firms, where he saw firsthand the challenges of manual workflows for due diligence, and decided to build a solution.

"&AI allows patent teams to replace manual workflows with AI so they can do due diligence in a fraction of the time and with much higher accuracy," said Harris. "In the process, we free up valuable attorney time for less mundane work."

Investor Kulveer Taggar said, "Caleb and Herbie have created something revolutionary. They've built an AI agent, Andy, that thinks and adapts like a patent attorney. The rapid adoption by AM Law 100 firms validates that this is the breakthrough the industry has been waiting for."

Andy functions as a proactive partner in patent law, adapting dynamically to new information and autonomously deciding how to use tools and resources to complete tasks. Unlike traditional workflows, where AI follows predetermined steps, Andy independently plans and adjusts its approach in real time.

Using the &AI platform, Andy can:

  • Quickly draft detailed claim charts, reducing hours of work to minutes.
  • Perform comprehensive prior art searches, improving accuracy and saving time.
  • Respond to office actions, using technical context and client goals.
  • Provide expert-level answers to project and subject-matter questions on demand.

To learn more visit https://www.tryandai.com/.

About &AI

&AI provides the first AI agent for patent attorneys. The agent Andy can navigate &AI’s platform for patent prosecution and litigation to help streamline workflows such as prior art searching and claim charting. Over time the company will expand to provide comprehensive solutions for discovery, prosecution and litigation. Founded by a team with deep AI and legal knowledge, &AI is helping law firms perform due diligence in a fraction of the time and with much higher accuracy, freeing up valuable attorney time for other tasks. Learn more at tryandai.com.

Media Contact:

Michelle Faulkner

Big Swing

617-510-6998

michelle@big-swing.com

data.title: Market Chatter: OpenAI, SoftBank, Oracle JV Eyes More Data Centers in Texas
data.description:
A joint venture between Microsoft-backed OpenAI, SoftBank Group and Oracle is close to choosing additional sites for data centers in Texas, while also considering more projects in other states, Bloomberg reported Thursday, citing an OpenAI spokesperson.

The JV, called Stargate, is aiming to spend billions of dollars to amp up the artificial intelligence infrastructure in the US, the news outlet reported.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Tyson Foods: Board Reduced to 13 Directors, Nine Independent >TSN
Tyson Foods Announces Results of Annual Meeting of Shareholders
data.description:
SPRINGDALE, Ark., Feb. 06, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Tyson Foods, Inc. announced the results of its Annual Meeting of Shareholders, which was held today.

Shareholders reelected 13 incumbent directors, with each receiving strong shareholder support according to preliminary voting results. Jonathan Mariner, having expressed his preference not to be renominated to the Board, was not put forward as a director nominee. This reduced the Board size to 13 directors, nine of which are independent. Kate Quinn was elected Chair of the Audit Committee.

“As we mark the 90th anniversary of Tyson Foods, we reflect on the decades of hard work that have transformed our business into a global, world-class food company,” said Chairman John H. Tyson. “I am confident we are working on the right things at the right time with the right effort every day to meet our goals. I’d like to thank our dedicated team members—and all the communities in which they live and work—as well as our shareholders for their ongoing support.”

As noted in the company’s most recent proxy statement, the board members elected today included: Chairman John H. Tyson, Les R. Baledge, Mike Beebe, Maria Claudia Borras, David J. Bronczek, Donnie King, Maria N. Martinez, Kevin M. McNamara, Cheryl S. Miller, Kate B. Quinn, Jeffrey K. Schomburger, Barbara A. Tyson and Noel White.

Additionally, shareholders ratified the selection of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for the 2025 fiscal year and voted against the sole shareholder proposal put forward this year.

About Tyson Foods, Inc. 

Tyson Foods, Inc. is a world-class food company and recognized leader in protein. Founded in 1935 by John W. Tyson, it has grown under four generations of family leadership. The Company is unified by this purpose: Tyson Foods. We Feed the World Like Family™ and has a broad portfolio of iconic products and brands including Tyson®, Jimmy Dean®, Hillshire Farm®, Ball Park®, Wright®, State Fair®, Aidells® and ibp®. Tyson Foods is dedicated to bringing high-quality food to every table in the world, safely and affordably, now and for future generations. Headquartered in Springdale, Arkansas, the company had approximately 138,000 team members as of September 2024. Visit www.tysonfoods.com. 

Media Contact: Laura Burns | Laura.Burns2@tyson.com | 479-466-0401

Category: IR

Source: Tyson Foods

data.title: Richardson Electronics CFO Sells 2,500 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameBen Robert J
RelationshipCFO, CAO, Corporate Secretary
TypeSell
Amount$33,192
SEC FilingForm 4

Ben Robert J, CFO, CAO, and Corporate Secretary of Richardson Electronics, Ltd., sold 2,500 shares of Common Stock on February 4, 2025, at a weighted average price of $13.277 per share, totaling $33,192. Following the transaction, Ben Robert J directly owns 54,534 shares of the company.

SEC Filing: RICHARDSON ELECTRONICS, LTD. [ RELL ] - Form 4 - Feb. 06, 2025

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 1 PM ET
data.description:
Year-End Gains in Productivity Weaken. What It Means for Growth.

Labor productivity grew at a rate of 1.2% in the fourth quarter of 2024. Growth was 2.3% in the third quarter, revised data showed.

Bank of England Cuts Rates, Warns of Tariff Impact

The central bank responded to a troubling mix of stuttering economic growth, stubborn inflation and President Trump's tariff threats.

China's Firms Are Bleeding Cash-and Vulnerable to Trump's Trade War

Companies across the country are bleeding cash as they struggle with overcapacity and weak spending in a slumping economy, leaving them vulnerable to tariffs.

Jobless claims rise slightly, but no sign of trouble brewing in labor market

The number of people who applied for unemployment benefits in early February rose slightly but remained at very low levels in a sign the labor market is still quite healthy.

Dow Rises and Pound Sinks After Bank of England Cuts Rates

Stocks rose slightly after higher-than-expected weekly unemployment claims and amid a flurry of earnings reports.

Eurozone Shoppers Spent Less as 2024 Ended Amid High Savings Caution

Retailers sold 0.2% less in December than November, a little worse than forecast, as consumers continued to save rather than splurge against a chilly economic backdrop.

German Factory Orders Get Late-2024 Boost as Tariffs Loom

Manufacturing orders in Germany rose in December on aerospace demand, although any tentative signs of a recovery for the struggling industrial sector face the imminent threat of tariffs from the U.S.

Hawkish BOJ Board Member Calls for Potentially Faster Rate Hikes

In a speech to business leaders, Naoki Tamura said that the central bank should raise rates to 1% or higher in the fiscal half starting in October.

Power Stocks Had Been on an AI Tear. Then Came DeepSeek.

The efficiency gains of DeepSeek's AI models startled the power industry, which is set to invest billions in electricity projects.

XRP Falls, Bitcoin Rises. The Crypto Tariff Fallout Is Mixed.

Altcoins haven't recovered fully from Monday's selloff.
